Besides Qiao's Compound, County another famous compound located east street, Qu's Compound.
Being built during reign Qianlong during Qing Dynasty (1644 1911), this 240-rooms compound history dating back over years covers area over 5300 square meters (6340 square yards); only one-fifth family's estate.
family outstanding representative Shanxi Merchants their estate used occupy almost entire eastern half County.

Upon entering, will find that each door higher than former each courtyard wider than last.
This because there saying that exceptional people come forth walking from high from narrow wide.

times, businessmen usually carved small beasts crouching with their mouths closed, their roofs.
These beasts implied that people were doing business should work down-to-earth speak little that their fortunes would through extraneous talking.
Qu's compound will find mouths these beasts open.

open-mouthed beasts symbolized that they were government.

biggest stage theater civilian residence also found Qu's Compound, which many ways proved wealth family.

South Adelaide you'll find Australia's Fleurie wine region.
There's special vineyard there, Stranger's Reach, where vines irrigation, their grapes ripening Australian summer with breezes from Great Southern Ocean cool them night.
Handpicked, traditional basket presses squeeze every ounce fantastic flavour from grapes after fermentation enjoy lavish months French oak.
Superb itself, with full-flavoured dish think it's drinking well already, could cellar this another five years with ease.
Wonderfully rich, supple for.
think Australian wines pretty much same, really should treat yourself bottle this delicious Cabernet Sauvignon wine.
Coming from small Fleurie Peninsula region, it's powerhouse ripe blackcurrant, blueberry flavours showing just good Aussie wine
"Wow, this packs wallop.
It's just voluminous, silky rich abundance cassis aromas, there's chocolate plum, cedary herbs, black olive graphite, powerhouse nose that takes prisoners.
palate, course, concentrated powerful wine, there's interesting cherryish kirsch-like higher-toned edge black fruit too, firm, chalk-dry tannins more cherry acidity.
This needs some robust foods steak maybe right now, will cellar many years suspect"
vineyards South Australia produce half Australia's total volume wine, with regions diverse lower altitude Barossa Valley, producing rich, structured reds, higher altitude vineyards such Adelaide Hills Eden Valley regions, perfect production crisp, whites.
Burgers wine match made heaven don't forget blue cheese element which could scupper your wine choice.
contrast creamy, salty notes cheese, head full, fruity reds with good acidity.
classic Aussie Cabernet Sauvignon will pair beautifully.
robust, flavoursome Portuguese pork stew that's faint-hearted, this needs full, ripe wine stand porky flavours.
World reds such Shiraz Cabernet.
Dandelion Vineyard's Pride Fleurieu Cabernet Sauvignon will definitely bill.
Lamb classic match Cabernet Sauvignon.
balanced tannins cedary flavours wine perfectly matching this simple, easy roast.

leaving stay traditional Japanese farm house near Miyama with beautiful vegetarian Buddhist couple, Keisan Setsusan.
spartan rustic (sleeping floor mats shared outhouse made appreciate what left behind) wonderful beautiful Japanese countryside.
family lives organic garden, where they make their tofu from their beans, their ancient rice strain, Japanese vegetables, some fruits homemade plum juice that like eating pure magic), chickens their eggs (never meat).
helped pound rice into gelatinous paste mochi, brandy sliced cooked kitchen with Setsusan, kids fished creek fish which brought huge smile Keisan every time they pulled squiggly fish, hiked through beautiful cedar forest that reminded hiking through redwoods California coast, went prayed their Buddhist temple asked that pray world peace), rang monastery bell, smiled with wonderful hosts entire time.
Meals were event, where would arrange many small plates bowls very strict fashion kept worrying that were placing chopsticks wrong side), turned zippers floor cushions away from home's altar, waited patiently grandfather lead blessing give thanks blessing meal.
food 'interesting' with many pickled vegetables, tofu mixes, soups, always rice.
Flavors textures like we've never experienced, some them were good.
night, they pulled some traditional games where blindfolded ourselves with help partner tried reassemble face board funny watch.
Japanese society struggling with consequences aging population (low birth rates mean population aging shrinking) combined with urbanization million people live Tokyo!)
which leaves empty homes countryside dying villages (literally figuratively) homes with amazing history, land, architecture that nearly free because wants them.
loved countryside people (almost elderly) Japanese farm land.
home stopped beautiful temples shrines.
first temple went Jingo-Ji Temple.
Before even temple, climb many steps.
first flight easy them then went
felt like could've flown airplane there.
PHEW!!
WORK KIDS THREE GROWN UPS!!
Then only then went into temple.
take shoes into temple.
After went inside, went pond large amount tadpoles.
Ginkakuji Temple really small sand clay replica
Fuji Silver Pagoda.
walked around path through garden walked quite bit.
Really, didn't much that temple, really beautiful, even rain.
Heian Shrine also pretty awesome really BIG.
favorite part when some bread feed gigantic cute, little turtles.
Brayden said could have stayed there hours.
wanted turtles food, because they were slow kept stealing from turtles.
Sometimes would even throw bread onto their shells, they didn't notice.
really funny.
Omri, tour guide, told story about ancient Japanese Shinto treasures.
treasures sword.
This story: There once eight daughters.
Seven eight daughters were kidnapped eight-headed serpent.
When serpent stuck head into gate drink sake, Susanoo slammed first gate head chopped off.
saved daughter while fighting beast, discovered sacred sword returned Shinto Goddess.
felt really happy when were saying good-bye people stayed with farmhouse, also felt little sad, because were leaving such nice people were always smiling, telling stories really full happiness.
temples shrine were pretty, especially rain.
really loved Japanese gardens, they made feel peaceful.
Brookie...what wonderful story...like you, found myself feeling sorry poor turtles!!
glad took such good care them.
also found myself wondering just what will doing next...'can't wait hear!
